title: crataegus monogyna subsp. calycina
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. calycina, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a subspecies of the Crataegus monogyna species, a deciduous tree native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The leaves, flowers, and fruits of Crataegus monogyna subsp. calycina have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing cognitive function, as well as providing relief from anxiety and insomnia. The herbal preparations of Crataegus monogyna subsp. calycina include teas, infusions, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to support heart health and overall well-being.
